asana.name: Asana
asana.short_description: A work management platform for organizing, tracking, and collaborating on projects with unlimited tasks, list/board/timeline/calendar views, and AI Studio.

features.description: List, board, timeline, and calendar views of the same underlying tasks.
features.title: Multiple project views

features.description: Asana's AI feature set, included on paid tiers with a monthly credit allowance.
features.title: AI Studio

features.description: AI Teammates, Timesheets & Budgets, Compliance Management, and Permissions Management available as add-ons to any plan.
features.title: Modular enterprise add-ons

pros: Enterprise capabilities (AI Teammates, Timesheets & Budgets, Compliance) available as add-ons rather than forcing a jump to the full Enterprise tier
Same task data viewable as list, board, timeline, or calendar without duplicating information
cons: Free plan is capped at 2 users, so real team use requires a paid tier immediately

faqs.answer: Yes — the Personal plan is free but limited to 2 users, aimed at individuals rather than teams.
faqs.question: Is Asana free for individuals?

faqs.answer: AI Studio is included on paid tiers (Starter and above) with a monthly AI credit allowance.
faqs.question: Does Asana include AI features on all plans?

qloapps.name: qloapps
qloapps.short_description: QloApps is free and open-source software that launches a multilingual/multicurrency hotel reservation and booking Website within a few minutes. QloApps allows you to take and manage both on-desk and online hotel bookings.

features.description: Combines a direct booking engine with a full property management system.
features.title: Booking engine + PMS

features.description: Available alongside the free self-hosted edition.
features.title: Optional managed cloud hosting

pros: Publishes real customer case studies from hotels using the platform
Offers both free self-hosting and optional managed cloud hosting

faqs.answer: Yes — the self-hosted edition is free and open source; an optional managed cloud hosting service is also available.
faqs.question: Is QloApps free to use?

huginn.name: Huginn

features.description: One agent's output can trigger another, composing complex automations from simple building blocks.
features.title: Chainable agents

pros: Agents can be chained together, letting complex automations be built from simple, individually-testable pieces

faqs.answer: Yes — Huginn lets you build agents that monitor the web and act, running entirely on infrastructure you control.
faqs.question: Is Huginn a self-hosted alternative to IFTTT or Zapier?

expensify.name: Expensify
expensify.short_description: Expensify handles receipt scanning, expense reports, corporate cards, travel booking, and bill pay for individuals, freelancers, and businesses.
expensify.starting_price: Free for individuals; business plans start at roughly $5/user/month

features.description: OCR technology reads a photographed or forwarded receipt and automatically fills in the merchant, date, amount, and expense category.
features.title: SmartScan receipt capture

features.description: A corporate charge card with real-time spend controls for admins; card usage can offset the cost of paid subscription plans.
features.title: Expensify Card

features.description: Multi-level approval chains and spending policies that route expense reports to the right approver before reimbursement.
features.title: Configurable approval workflows

features.description: GPS-based mileage logging that calculates reimbursable distance and rate automatically.
features.title: Mileage tracking

features.description: Expense capture and reimbursement across different currencies for teams with international travel or contractors.
features.title: Multi-currency support

features.description: Integrated flight, hotel, and car booking with company travel policy compliance built into the booking flow.
features.title: Expensify Travel

features.description: An accounts payable tool that lets finance teams pay vendor invoices directly from the platform.
features.title: Bill Pay

features.description: Lets freelancers and businesses send client invoices and track when they're paid.
features.title: Invoicing

features.description: Two-way sync with QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, and other accounting systems.
features.title: Accounting integrations

features.description: Gives admins visibility into spend by employee, department, or project as transactions come in.
features.title: Real-time reporting dashboard

pros: SmartScan meaningfully cuts down manual data entry for receipts and expense reports.
The free plan gives individuals and freelancers a genuinely usable no-cost option for basic tracking.
Combines expense reporting, a corporate card, travel booking, and bill pay instead of requiring several separate vendors.
Broad accounting software integrations ease month-end close for finance teams.
The mobile apps are polished and make on-the-go expense submission fast and simple.
cons: The plan structure (individual free tier versus Collect and Control) and card-linked discounts can be confusing to evaluate up front.
Customer support has drawn user complaints about slow response times, especially for account and billing issues.
SmartScan OCR occasionally misreads handwritten or low-quality receipts, requiring manual correction.
Configuring approval workflows and expense policies has a learning curve for admins setting the system up.
Teams needing deep ERP-style financial controls beyond expenses and travel may find the feature set narrower than a dedicated enterprise finance platform.

faqs.answer: Yes. Expensify offers a free plan aimed at individuals tracking their own expenses. Paid Collect and Control plans add team approval workflows, card programs, and deeper accounting integrations for businesses.
faqs.question: Is Expensify free?

faqs.answer: SmartScan is Expensify's OCR-based receipt scanning feature. It automatically reads a photographed or forwarded receipt and fills in the merchant, date, amount, and category so users don't have to enter it manually.
faqs.question: What is SmartScan?

faqs.answer: Yes. Expensify connects with QuickBooks Online and Desktop, Xero, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, and other accounting systems to sync expense data with the general ledger.
faqs.question: Does Expensify integrate with accounting software?

faqs.answer: The Expensify Card is a corporate charge card issued through Expensify that gives admins real-time spend controls. Its transactions can also offset the cost of paid subscription plans.
faqs.question: What is the Expensify Card?

faqs.answer: Yes. Expensify has traded on the Nasdaq under the ticker EXFY since its 2021 IPO.
faqs.question: Is Expensify a public company?

features: SmartScan receipt capture for one person
A capped number of free SmartScans per month
Expense report submission and basic approval workflow
pricing_plans.price: From approximately $5/user/month with the Expensify Card discount

features: Custom expense policies and domain-level admin controls
Deeper ERP integrations such as NetSuite and Sage Intacct
Expensify Travel and Bill Pay access
pricing_plans.price: From approximately $9/user/month with the Expensify Card discount

make.name: Make
make.short_description: Make is a visual no-code platform for building, automating, and integrating workflows that connect apps, data, and AI logic.
make.starting_price: Free (paid plans from around $9/month billed annually)

features.description: Build automations as a flowchart on a drag-and-drop canvas, making complex, branching logic easy to follow and edit.
features.title: Visual scenario builder

features.description: Connect popular CRMs, spreadsheets, email tools, e-commerce platforms, and more through pre-built modules.
features.title: Thousands of app integrations

features.description: Use generic HTTP requests, webhooks, and custom API calls to integrate services that don't have a native connector.
features.title: Custom API and webhook modules

features.description: Split a single scenario into multiple conditional paths so different data can trigger different downstream actions.
features.title: Routers and branching logic

features.description: Iterators, aggregators, and built-in functions reshape, filter, and combine data as it flows between apps.
features.title: Data mapping and transformation tools

features.description: Attach error handlers, retry logic, and fallback routes to scenarios so failures don't silently break a workflow.
features.title: Error handling and retries

features.description: Start from a library of pre-built templates for common automation patterns instead of building from scratch.
features.title: Scenario templates

features.description: Inspect past runs and data bundles at each step to debug issues and understand exactly what happened in a scenario.
features.title: Execution logs and history

features.description: Incorporate large language model steps and AI agent building blocks directly into automation workflows.
features.title: AI modules and agents

features.description: Run scenarios on a schedule or trigger them instantly via webhooks for near real-time automation.
features.title: Scheduling and instant triggers

pros: Visual, flowchart-style builder makes complex multi-branch automations easier to understand than list-based tools
Very wide reach through native integrations plus generic HTTP and webhook modules for almost any API
Strong data mapping and transformation tools for reshaping information between apps
Free plan and template library make it accessible to try before committing to a paid plan
Detailed execution logs and error-handling options support serious debugging and reliability
cons: Steeper learning curve than simpler, wizard-driven automation tools for first-time users
Usage-based operations/credits pricing can make costs less predictable for high-volume workflows
Some advanced concepts (bundles, iterators, aggregators) require a bit of trial and error to master
Very deep, vendor-certified integrations for specific enterprise systems may be thinner than dedicated point solutions
Complex scenarios with many modules can become visually cluttered and harder to navigate on the canvas

faqs.answer: Yes, Make offers a free plan with a limited monthly allowance of operations/credits, which is enough for light automation use. Paid plans remove those limits and add features like higher execution priority and more credits.
faqs.question: Is Make free to use?

faqs.answer: Make was previously known as Integromat. It was founded in 2012, acquired by Celonis in 2020, and rebranded as Make in 2022 as part of a broader repositioning of the product.
faqs.question: What was Make called before?

faqs.answer: No, Make is designed as a no-code visual builder, so most automations can be created by dragging and connecting modules. That said, comfort with concepts like JSON and APIs helps when working with custom HTTP requests or advanced data mapping.
faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Make?

faqs.answer: Both are automation platforms, but Make emphasizes a visual, flowchart-style canvas that shows branching logic and data flow directly, while Zapier traditionally favors a simpler, more linear step-by-step setup.
faqs.question: How is Make different from Zapier?

faqs.answer: Yes, Make is built for scenarios with routers, loops, error handlers, and multiple branching paths, which makes it well suited to more elaborate workflows beyond simple one-to-one app connections.
faqs.question: Can Make handle complex, multi-step business processes?

features: Access to the visual scenario builder
Access to thousands of app integrations
Multi-user access with team roles and permissions
Shared scenario templates across a team
pricing_plans.price: From around $29/month (Teams); custom pricing for Enterprise

quickbooks-online.name: QuickBooks Online
quickbooks-online.short_description: Cloud accounting software for small businesses covering invoicing, expenses, payroll, and financial reporting from Intuit.
quickbooks-online.starting_price: From approximately $35/month (Simple Start plan, US pricing; promotions and regional pricing vary)

features.description: Create, send, and track customer invoices and quotes, with automatic reminders for unpaid balances.
features.title: Invoicing and estimates

features.description: Capture receipts and categorize expenses, including automatic import from connected bank and credit card feeds.
features.title: Expense and receipt tracking

features.description: Connect financial accounts to automatically import and match transactions against the books.
features.title: Bank feed reconciliation

features.description: Generate profit and loss, balance sheet, cash flow, and other standard reports for owners, lenders, and tax preparers.
features.title: Financial reporting

features.description: Run payroll, calculate taxes, and manage direct deposit through an integrated Intuit payroll subscription.
features.title: Payroll add-on

features.description: Assign different permission levels to employees, bookkeepers, and accountants working in the same company file.
features.title: Multi-user roles and accountant access

features.description: Automatically log business mileage via the mobile app for expense reporting and tax deductions.
features.title: Mileage tracking

features.description: Track quantities on hand, cost of goods sold, and reorder points for product-based businesses on eligible plans.
features.title: Inventory management

features.description: Track income and expenses by project, location, or class to see profitability across different parts of the business.
features.title: Project and class tracking

features.description: Integrate with e-commerce, point-of-sale, and CRM apps, or build custom connections through Intuit's developer API.
features.title: App marketplace and API access

pros: Wide adoption among small businesses and accountants, making it easy to find bookkeepers already familiar with the platform
Scales across four plan tiers, so pricing and features can grow with the business
Strong bank feed automation and standard financial reporting for owners and tax preparers
Mobile apps for iOS and Android cover invoicing, receipt capture, and mileage tracking on the go
Large ecosystem of third-party integrations and an API for connecting to other business tools
cons: Monthly subscription costs add up once payroll, payments, or higher tiers are included
Has a learning curve for users without prior bookkeeping or accounting experience
Some users report inconsistent customer support response quality
Lower-tier plans limit the number of users and access to features like inventory or project tracking
Pricing has increased over time, which has drawn criticism from long-time subscribers

faqs.answer: QuickBooks Online is a cloud-based, browser-accessible subscription product with continuous updates, while QuickBooks Desktop is locally installed software typically purchased as an annual license, often preferred by users who want offline access or more advanced industry-specific features.
faqs.question: What is the difference between QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop?

faqs.answer: Yes, Intuit provides a built-in data conversion tool to import company files, including lists, transactions, and balances, from QuickBooks Desktop into QuickBooks Online, though some complex data may require manual review after migration.
faqs.question: Can I migrate existing data from QuickBooks Desktop to QuickBooks Online?

faqs.answer: Payroll is not included in the core plans by default; it is offered as a separate QuickBooks Payroll add-on subscription that integrates directly with the accounting data.
faqs.question: Does QuickBooks Online include payroll?

faqs.answer: Yes, Intuit offers QuickBooks Online mobile apps for iOS and Android that support invoicing, expense and receipt capture, mileage tracking, and viewing account balances.
faqs.question: Is there a mobile app for QuickBooks Online?

faqs.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online supports dedicated accountant user access, allowing an external bookkeeper or CPA to log in with their own credentials and permissions to review or adjust the books remotely.
faqs.question: Can my accountant access my QuickBooks Online account?

pros: Easy to set up and use, even for creators with no technical background
Combines courses, downloads, memberships, coaching, and email marketing in one place
Free plan lets new creators start selling before paying anything
No transaction fees on paid plans
Responsive, helpful customer support
cons: Lacks advanced LMS features like certifications and SCORM compliance
Website and email design customization is more limited than dedicated tools
Free plan charges a transaction fee on sales
No native mobile app for creators or students
Advanced marketing automation is thinner than dedicated email platforms

faqs.answer: Yes, Podia offers a free plan that lets creators sell digital downloads and limited products, though it includes a per-sale transaction fee that paid plans remove or reduce.
faqs.question: Does Podia have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Podia supports selling online courses, digital downloads, memberships, coaching sessions, and webinars, all managed from a single dashboard.
faqs.question: What can you sell on Podia?

faqs.answer: The free plan charges a transaction fee on sales, while Podia's paid plans remove or significantly reduce that fee depending on the tier.
faqs.question: Does Podia charge transaction fees?

faqs.answer: Podia provides migration guidance and support for importing existing courses, student lists, and email subscribers from other platforms.
faqs.question: Can I migrate my existing courses and students to Podia?

faqs.answer: Podia does not offer a dedicated native mobile app; the platform is designed to be used through a web browser on both desktop and mobile devices.
faqs.question: Is there a Podia mobile app?

axiom.name: Axiom
axiom.short_description: Axiom is a fully managed, schema-less log and event data platform built for petabyte-scale observability with usage-based pricing.

features.description: Store event data without predefined schemas, using query-time virtual fields for transformations.
features.title: Schema-less ingestion

features.description: A piped, sequential query syntax for logs, traces, metrics and events.
features.title: APL query language

features.description: 95%+ compression lets teams retain complete datasets without prohibitive storage costs.
features.title: High-ratio compression

features.description: AI agents can query observability data directly using APL, the same primitives engineers use.
features.title: Native MCP support

features.description: Run APL queries from within Splunk via a dedicated app.
features.title: Splunk integration

features.description: Built-in visualization and alerting on top of ingested data.
features.title: Dashboards, alerts and metrics

pros: Permanent free tier with no credit card required
Single usage-based pricing dial across logs, traces, metrics and events, with no SKU matrix
Native OpenTelemetry, Vector, Fluent Bit and AWS Kinesis Firehose integrations
AI agents can query data using the same APL primitives as engineers
cons: Enterprise features (SSO, RBAC, audit log, directory sync) are paid add-ons even on the Cloud plan
Free tier is capped at 25GB storage and 30-day retention

faqs.answer: Yes, a permanent Personal plan with 500GB/month data loading and 30-day retention, no credit card required.
faqs.question: Does Axiom have a free plan?

faqs.answer: The Axiom Cloud plan charges a $25/month platform fee plus usage-based charges, with automatic volume discounts.
faqs.question: How is Axiom priced beyond the free tier?

faqs.answer: Yes, via native MCP support so AI agents can query observability data using APL.
faqs.question: Does Axiom support AI agents?

faqs.answer: Yes, via a dedicated Splunk app for running APL queries.
faqs.question: Can Axiom integrate with Splunk?

Enterprise security features (SOC 2, GDPR, SSO, RBAC) included on all plans, not gated to top tiers
Positioned as lower-cost than Zapier, Make, and n8n
cons: Usage-based CPU-second billing can make cost estimation less predictable than flat-rate plans
The Managed Service tier starts at $199/month for hands-on implementation support
Legally registered as Nocode LTD with limited public company background information

faqs.answer: Yes, Latenode offers a free plan with 10,000 CPU seconds and 5 active workflows per month.

faqs.answer: No, Latenode provides access to 1,200+ AI models and apps, including GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini, without requiring separate API credentials.
faqs.question: Do I need my own AI API keys?

faqs.answer: Paid usage is billed by CPU seconds, for example approximately $10.80/month for 100,000 CPU seconds, plus a Managed Service tier starting at $199/month.
faqs.question: How is Latenode priced beyond the free plan?

faqs.answer: Yes, Latenode supports full custom JavaScript code inside workflow nodes.
faqs.question: Can I write custom code in Latenode workflows?

Pro features (branding, guest roles, advanced views) require a paid license
Hosted plans bill per-user with additional charges for extra storage and guests

faqs.answer: Yes, the Community edition is free to self-host under Planka's Fair Use license, though it has some feature restrictions compared to Pro.
faqs.question: Is Planka free?

faqs.answer: Yes, Planka offers managed cloud hosting for both the Community and Pro editions, priced per month plus per-user fees.
faqs.question: Does Planka offer a hosted/cloud version?

faqs.answer: Yes, Planka's source code is publicly available on GitHub under a fair-code licensing model.
faqs.question: Is Planka open source?

faqs.answer: Yes, Planka is commonly deployed via Docker, with over 8.1 million image pulls reported.
faqs.question: Can I self-host Planka with Docker?

features: No calendar/map views, recurring cards, or custom branding

shopware.name: Shopware
shopware.short_description: Shopware is a German ecommerce platform with a free open-source Community Edition and paid plans from around €600/month.

features.description: AI-assisted commerce management built into the platform.
features.title: Shopware Intelligence Copilot

features.description: Built-in content management, search visibility, and data-analysis tools.
features.title: Integrated CMS, SEO, and analytics

features.description: Visual tools for automation and custom business logic.
features.title: Flow Builder and Rule Builder

features.description: Enterprise-level features for business-to-business selling.
features.title: B2B components

features.description: Multi-channel sales capabilities across storefronts and channels.
features.title: Omnichannel connect

features.description: Specialized B2B sales environments available on higher tiers.
features.title: Digital sales rooms

features.description: Integrated payment processing within the platform.
features.title: Shopware Payments

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition under the MIT license
Flexible deployment options: self-hosted, SaaS, or PaaS
Large partner ecosystem with 1,200+ certified agencies
Strong B2B feature set alongside B2C capabilities
Named a Visionary in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce
cons: Top-tier Beyond plan requires a custom sales quote
Pricing is tied to gross merchandise value (GMV) and other business factors, not fully transparent
Evolve and Beyond tiers are enterprise-priced (€2,400+/month)
Full AI feature set (Shopware Intelligence+) costs extra (€19–€29/month)

faqs.answer: Shopware Community Edition is free and open source under the MIT license. Paid Rise, Evolve, and Beyond plans add cloud hosting and additional features.
faqs.question: Is Shopware free?

faqs.answer: Rise starts from €600/month, Evolve from €2,400/month (both excl. VAT), and Beyond is custom-priced.
faqs.question: How much does Shopware cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is open source and MIT licensed.
faqs.question: Is Shopware open source?

faqs.answer: Shopware is headquartered in Germany.
faqs.question: Where is Shopware based?

kill-bill.name: Kill Bill
kill-bill.short_description: Open-source subscription billing and payments platform you self-host for full data control.

features.description: Supports usage-based, prepaid, postpaid, and multi-tenant subscription models.
features.title: Subscription billing engine

features.description: Native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.
features.title: Payment gateway plugins

features.description: A back-office web interface for managing subscriptions and payments.
features.title: Kaui admin UI

features.description: An extensible framework for building custom integrations and modifications.
features.title: Plugin architecture

features.description: Connects to tax and compliance engines such as Avalara and Vertex.
features.title: Tax engine integrations

features.description: Deploys via Docker or Kubernetes in your own infrastructure, avoiding SaaS lock-in.
features.title: Self-hosted deployment

pros: Core platform is free and fully open source under Apache 2.0
Full data ownership since it runs in your own infrastructure
No per-transaction or revenue-percentage fees
Long track record, in production for over 15 years
Plugin architecture supports many payment gateways and tax engines
cons: Requires engineering resources to deploy, operate, and maintain
No official low-code hosted option from the core project itself (managed hosting is via third parties)
Enterprise accelerator (Aviate) is a separate proprietary offering, not part of the free core

faqs.answer: Yes, the core Kill Bill platform is open source under the Apache License 2.0 and free to self-host.
faqs.question: Is Kill Bill free?

faqs.answer: Kill Bill is designed to be self-hosted via Docker or Kubernetes; third-party providers such as ChaChing offer managed hosting.
faqs.question: Do I need to host Kill Bill myself?

faqs.answer: It has native plugin support for Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, Braintree, and other gateways.
faqs.question: What payment gateways does Kill Bill support?

faqs.answer: The codebase is primarily Java, with smaller portions in JavaScript, CSS, Shell, and PL/pgSQL.
faqs.question: What language is Kill Bill written in?
